scope: units of force, mass, acceleration, velocity, momentum
out_of_scope: A-Level vector resolution beyond scalar treatment
classification_hints: question involves forces, acceleration, velocity-time graphs, Newton's laws, moments or momentum
common_misconceptions: a force is needed to keep an object moving at constant velocity
heavier objects always fall faster in a vacuum

common_misclassifications.reason: both may involve work and energy; Topic 1 focuses on forces and motion, Topic 4 on energy transfers and resources

out_of_scope: vector notation beyond scalar treatment required at IGCSE
classification_hints: question asks to interpret or sketch motion graphs
question uses equations of motion
common_misconceptions: the slope of a distance-time graph gives acceleration

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve motion; 1.1 is kinematics, 1.2 is dynamics and forces

scope: Newton's first, second and third laws
out_of_scope: coefficient of friction calculations beyond syllabus
classification_hints: question asks about resultant force, Newton's laws or terminal velocity
common_misconceptions: mass and weight are the same quantity

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve forces; 1.2 focuses on linear motion, 1.3 on turning effects

out_of_scope: couples and torque beyond simple moment calculations
classification_hints: question involves pivots, levers, balance or centre of mass
common_misconceptions: the moment depends only on force, not perpendicular distance

scope: safety features reducing force in collisions
classification_hints: question asks to calculate momentum before/after collision
question links crumple zones, seat belts or air bags to force reduction
common_misconceptions: momentum is always lost in a collision

common_misclassifications.reason: both use F=ma; momentum questions focus on mv before and after impact

scope: units of current, charge, potential difference, resistance, power, energy
out_of_scope: internal resistance circuits beyond simple treatment
classification_hints: question involves circuits, current, voltage, resistance or mains electricity
common_misconceptions: current is used up in a circuit
resistance is always constant regardless of temperature

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve current and magnetic fields; Topic 2 is circuit electricity, Topic 6 is magnetism and electromagnetism

scope: current as rate of flow of charge
classification_hints: question asks for charge transferred or links current to charge flow
common_misconceptions: electrons flow from positive to negative terminal

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve circuit quantities; 2.1 focuses on charge/current, 2.2 on voltage/resistance

classification_hints: question asks to calculate V, I or R
question involves Ohm's law or I-V graphs
common_misconceptions: resistance depends on voltage or current

common_misclassifications.reason: both use V=IR; 2.2 is component behaviour, 2.3 is circuit arrangement

scope: current and voltage rules in series and parallel
total resistance in series and parallel
classification_hints: question involves series or parallel resistor arrangements
common_misconceptions: current is the same at all points in a parallel circuit

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve household or power contexts; 2.3 is circuit theory, 2.4 is mains electricity

scope: power and energy in domestic circuits
P = IV, P = I^2R, E = IVt
classification_hints: question mentions household circuits, plugs, fuses, kWh or mains supply
common_misconceptions: fuses protect people from electric shock

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve power calculations; 2.4 is electrical power, 4.3 is mechanical power

scope: magnetic field around a current-carrying wire
classification_hints: question asks about magnetic field produced by current
common_misconceptions: a current creates a magnetic field only inside the wire

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve current and magnetism; 2.5 is the magnetic field due to current, 6.2 is electromagnetism and motor effect

scope: units of wavelength, frequency, speed, amplitude
classification_hints: question involves wave properties, sound, light reflection/refraction or fibre optics
common_misconceptions: waves transfer matter as well as energy

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve light; Topic 3 is wave behaviour, Topic 8 is astrophysics and cosmology

scope: amplitude, wavelength, frequency, period, wave speed
classification_hints: question asks to define or calculate wave quantities
common_misconceptions: frequency depends on amplitude

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve waves; 3.1 is general wave properties, 3.2 is sound

classification_hints: question mentions sound, ultrasound, sonar or echo
common_misconceptions: sound can travel through a vacuum

common_misclassifications.reason: both use wave terminology; 3.2 focuses on sound applications

classification_hints: question involves mirrors, lenses, ray diagrams or refractive index
common_misconceptions: refraction only happens when light enters water at 90 degrees

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve light at boundaries; 3.3 is reflection/refraction, 3.4 is total internal reflection

classification_hints: question mentions critical angle, TIR or fibre optics
common_misconceptions: total internal reflection can occur when light travels from air to glass

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve light at boundaries; 3.4 is TIR and critical angle

classification_hints: question involves energy forms, conservation of energy, work, power or energy resources
common_misconceptions: energy is created or destroyed in a device

common_misclassifications.reason: both may use work and power; Topic 4 is energy, Topic 1 is forces and motion

classification_hints: question asks to identify energy forms or calculate efficiency
common_misconceptions: efficiency can exceed 100%

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve energy and work; 4.1 is general transfers, 4.2 is work and power

out_of_scope: vector dot product treatment of work
classification_hints: question asks to calculate work done or power
common_misconceptions: work is done when holding a heavy object stationary

common_misclassifications.reason: both use force; 4.2 calculates work/energy transfer, 1.2 calculates acceleration

scope: advantages and disadvantages of energy resources
classification_hints: question compares energy resources or describes power station energy transfers
common_misconceptions: renewable resources have no environmental impact

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve energy; 4.3 focuses on resources and generation

out_of_scope: kinetic theory derivation of gas laws
classification_hints: question involves density, pressure, state changes or particle model of matter
common_misconceptions: particles expand when heated

common_misclassifications.reason: both use particle ideas; Topic 5 is matter states, Topic 3 is waves

scope: pressure in solids, liquids and gases
classification_hints: question asks to calculate density or fluid pressure
common_misconceptions: pressure acts only downward in a fluid

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve particles and states; 5.1 is density/pressure, 5.2 is state changes

scope: particle model of solids, liquids and gases
classification_hints: question asks about melting, boiling, latent heat or particle arrangement
common_misconceptions: temperature changes during boiling at constant pressure

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve gas behaviour; 5.2 is state change, 5.3 is ideal gas model

out_of_scope: ideal gas equation pV = nRT with moles
classification_hints: question involves gas laws, absolute zero or Brownian motion
common_misconceptions: absolute zero is the temperature at which all motion stops

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve gas particles; 5.3 is gas laws, 5.2 is state changes

scope: units of magnetic flux density at IGCSE level
out_of_scope: Faraday's law quantitative derivations beyond syllabus
classification_hints: question involves magnets, magnetic fields, motors, generators or transformers
common_misconceptions: magnetic field lines start and stop on poles

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve current; Topic 6 is electromagnetic interactions, Topic 2 is circuit electricity

classification_hints: question asks about magnetic poles, field lines or magnetic materials
common_misconceptions: magnetic monopoles exist

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve magnetic fields; 6.1 is permanent magnets, 6.2 is electromagnetism

scope: magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor
classification_hints: question asks about force on a current-carrying wire in a magnetic field
common_misceptions: the force on a wire is independent of current direction

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve current producing magnetic fields; 6.2 focuses on motor force, 2.5 on field patterns

classification_hints: question involves moving conductors in magnetic fields, generators or transformers
common_misconceptions: transformers work with direct current

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve magnetic fields and current; 6.3 is induction/generation, 6.2 is motor effect

scope: units of activity, count rate, half-life
classification_hints: question involves radioactive decay, half-life, nuclear fission or fusion
common_misconceptions: radioactivity can be neutralised by chemical means

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve nuclear/astrophysical contexts; Topic 7 is radioactivity and nuclear energy, Topic 8 is astrophysics

classification_hints: question asks about types of radiation, half-life or safety
common_misconceptions: beta particles are electromagnetic radiation

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve nucleus; 7.1 is decay/radiation, 7.2 is fission/fusion

out_of_scope: binding energy per nucleon curve calculations
classification_hints: question mentions fission, fusion or chain reactions
common_misconceptions: fusion releases less energy than fission

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve nucleus; 7.2 is fission/fusion energy

classification_hints: question involves stars, galaxies, red-shift, Big Bang or stellar evolution
common_misconceptions: the Big Bang was an explosion in space

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve nuclear processes; Topic 8 is astrophysics/cosmology, Topic 7 is radioactivity

classification_hints: question asks about the Big Bang or expansion of the Universe
common_misconceptions: the Universe expanded into empty space

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve stars/Universe; 8.1 is cosmology, 8.2 is stellar lifecycle

scope: nebulae, protostars, main sequence, red giant, white dwarf
classification_hints: question describes stages of stellar evolution
common_misconceptions: all stars end as black holes

common_misclassifications.reason: both involve stars; 8.2 is lifecycle, 8.3 is red-shift/galaxies

classification_hints: question mentions red-shift, galaxy spectra or expansion evidence
common_misconceptions: red-shift means a galaxy is actually red in colour

common_misclassifications.reason: both use wave shift ideas; 8.3 is cosmological red-shift, 3.1 is general wave properties
